name: unifi
description: A Pulumi provider for managing a Ubiquiti UniFi deployment — the Network and Protect applications on a Dream Machine, and UniFi Drive on a UNAS appliance — via the local UniFi OS APIs.

apiKey.description: UniFi OS local API key. Preferred for headless automation. Mutually exclusive with username/password.

insecureTls.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (for self-signed controller and UNAS certs).

unasUrl.description: Base URL of the UNAS appliance hosting UniFi Drive, e.g. https://192.168.1.20. A SEPARATE host from `url`; required to manage `unifi:drive:*` resources.

unasUsername.description: Local UniFi OS admin username on the UNAS appliance (UniFi Drive has no API-key auth).

url.description: Base URL of the UniFi controller, e.g. https://192.168.1.1 (omit any /api suffix).

username.description: Local admin username. Use with password when not using an API key.

fallbackNetworkId.description: FallbackNetworkId is the fallback network for failed 802.1X auth (the network's `_id`).

behavior.description: Behavior of the lighting: breath | steady.

mode.description: Mode of the lighting animation: speed | network.

ifname.description: Ifname is the interface name, e.g. "eth1" (required key).

networkGroup.description: NetworkGroup is the assigned group, e.g. "LAN", "LAN2", "WAN", "WAN2".

brightness.description: Brightness is the front display brightness (1-100). Setting it overrides the global default.

idleTimeout.description: IdleTimeout is the front display idle timeout in seconds (10-3600). Setting it overrides the global default.

nightModeBegins.description: NightModeBegins is the night-mode start time, "HH:MM".

nightModeEnds.description: NightModeEnds is the night-mode end time, "HH:MM".

etherLighting.description: EtherLighting configures the EtherLighting LED strip (supported switches).

override.description: Override controls the status LED: default | on | off.

overrideColor.description: OverrideColor is the LED color as a hex string, e.g. "#0000ff".

enabled.description: Enabled enables PDU outlet control.

powerCycleEnabled.description: PowerCycleEnabled enables scheduled power cycling for PDU outlets.

cycleEnabled.description: CycleEnabled allows the outlet to be power-cycled.

index.description: Index is the 1-based outlet number (required key).

name.description: Name is the outlet label.

relayState.description: RelayState powers the outlet on (true) or off (false).

enum.name: Auto
enum.description: Automatic PoE based on device class.

aggregateNumPorts.description: AggregateNumPorts is the number of consecutive ports in a link aggregation group (1-8).

dot1xCtrl.description: Dot1xCtrl: auto | force_authorized | force_unauthorized | mac_based | multi_host.

dot1xIdleTimeout.description: Dot1xIdleTimeout is the 802.1X idle timeout in seconds.

egressRateLimitKbps.description: EgressRateLimitKbps caps egress bandwidth in kbps (requires egressRateLimitKbpsEnabled).

egressRateLimitKbpsEnabled.description: EgressRateLimitKbpsEnabled toggles the egress rate limit.

excludedNetworkIds.description: ExcludedNetworkIds lists tagged networks to block on the port (excluded_networkconf_ids).

fecMode.description: FecMode: rs-fec | fc-fec | default | disabled (for SFP+/SFP28 ports).

fullDuplex.description: FullDuplex forces full-duplex when autoneg is off.

isolation.description: Isolation enables port isolation (no traffic to other isolated ports).

lldpmedEnabled.description: LldpmedEnabled enables LLDP-MED on the port.

mirrorPortIdx.description: MirrorPortIdx is the source port to mirror when opMode is "mirror".

name.description: Name is the port label.

nativeNetworkId.description: NativeNetworkId is the untagged/native network for the port (native_networkconf_id).

portIdx.description: PortIdx is the 1-based physical port number (required key).

portKeepaliveEnabled.description: PortKeepaliveEnabled enables PoE keepalive for legacy powered devices.

portProfileId.description: PortProfileId applies a saved port profile (the profile's `_id`, sent as portconf_id).

portSecurityEnabled.description: PortSecurityEnabled restricts the port to specific MAC addresses.

portSecurityMacAddress.description: PortSecurityMacAddress lists the MACs permitted when portSecurityEnabled is true.

priorityQueue1Level.description: PriorityQueue1Level is the QoS priority-queue 1 level (0-100).

priorityQueue2Level.description: PriorityQueue2Level is the QoS priority-queue 2 level (0-100).

priorityQueue3Level.description: PriorityQueue3Level is the QoS priority-queue 3 level (0-100).

priorityQueue4Level.description: PriorityQueue4Level is the QoS priority-queue 4 level (0-100).

settingPreference.description: SettingPreference: auto (inherit profile) | manual (use these overrides).

stormctrlBroadcastLevel.description: StormctrlBroadcastLevel is the broadcast storm control level (0-100, percent).

stormctrlBroadcastRate.description: StormctrlBroadcastRate is the broadcast storm control rate (pps).

stormctrlMcastLevel.description: StormctrlMcastLevel is the multicast storm control level (0-100, percent).

stormctrlMcastRate.description: StormctrlMcastRate is the multicast storm control rate (pps).

stormctrlUcastLevel.description: StormctrlUcastLevel is the unknown-unicast storm control level (0-100, percent).

stormctrlUcastRate.description: StormctrlUcastRate is the unknown-unicast storm control rate (pps).

stpPortMode.description: StpPortMode enables spanning-tree on the port.

taggedVlanMgmt.description: TaggedVlanMgmt: auto | block_all | custom. With "custom", use excludedNetworkIds.

voiceNetworkId.description: VoiceNetworkId is the voice VLAN network for VoIP phones (voice_networkconf_id).

antennaGain.description: AntennaGain in dBi (for devices with configurable antennas).

channel.description: Channel is the radio channel, a number or "auto".

minRssi.description: MinRssi is the minimum RSSI (dBm, negative) below which clients are kicked. Setting it enables the feature unless minRssiEnabled is given.

minRssiEnabled.description: MinRssiEnabled toggles the minimum-RSSI kick feature.

name.description: Name is the controller's radio name (e.g. "wifi0").

radio.description: Radio band identifying which radio to tune: ng (2.4GHz) | na (5GHz) | ad (60GHz) | 6e (6GHz). Required key.

txPower.description: TxPower is the transmit power in dBm, or "auto". Honored only when txPowerMode is "custom".

txPowerMode.description: TxPowerMode: auto | medium | high | low | custom.

contact.description: Contact is the SNMP contact string.

location.description: Location is the SNMP location string.

priority.description: Priority is the spanning-tree bridge priority: a multiple of 4096 from 0 to 61440.

poeMode.description: PoeMode is the device-wide default PoE mode: auto | pasv24 | passthrough | off.

vlanEnabled.description: VlanEnabled enables 802.1Q VLAN switching on the device.

mode.description: Mode is the gateway VRRP role: primary | secondary.

enum.name: AddressGroup
enum.description: Group of IPv4 addresses or CIDRs.

enum.name: PortGroup
enum.description: Group of port numbers or ranges.

enum.name: Ipv6AddressGroup
enum.description: Group of IPv6 addresses or CIDRs.

enum.name: Reject
enum.description: Discard matching traffic and send a rejection.

established.description: Established matches packets in the established connection state.

invalid.description: Invalid matches packets in the invalid connection state.

new.description: New matches packets in the new connection state.

related.description: Related matches packets in the related connection state.

address.description: Address is the destination IPv4 address or CIDR to match.

addressIpv6.description: AddressIpv6 is the destination IPv6 address or CIDR to match.

firewallGroupIds.description: FirewallGroupIds are the destination firewall group IDs to match.

networkId.description: NetworkId is the destination network (firewall network conf) ID to match.

networkType.description: NetworkType selects how NetworkId is interpreted: ADDRv4 | NETv4.

port.description: Port is the destination port or port range to match.

enum.name: Addrv4
enum.description: Interpret the network ID as an IPv4 host address.

enum.name: Netv4
enum.description: Interpret the network ID as an IPv4 network.

icmpTypename.description: IcmpTypename restricts an icmp rule to a specific ICMP type, e.g. echo-request, destination-unreachable, time-exceeded.

icmpv6Typename.description: Icmpv6Typename restricts an icmpv6 rule to a specific ICMPv6 type, e.g. echo-request, packet-too-big, neighbor-solicitation.

matchExcepted.description: MatchExcepted inverts the protocol match (match all except the specified protocol).

protocol.description: Protocol the rule matches (IPv4 rulesets), e.g. all | tcp | udp | tcp_udp | icmp | a numeric protocol number, or empty for any.

protocolV6.description: ProtocolV6 the rule matches (IPv6 rulesets), e.g. all | tcp | udp | tcp_udp | icmpv6 | a numeric protocol number, or empty for any.

address.description: Address is the source IPv4 address or CIDR to match.

addressIpv6.description: AddressIpv6 is the source IPv6 address or CIDR to match.

firewallGroupIds.description: FirewallGroupIds are the source firewall group IDs to match.

mac.description: Mac is the source MAC address to match.

networkId.description: NetworkId is the source network (firewall network conf) ID to match.

networkType.description: NetworkType selects how NetworkId is interpreted: ADDRv4 | NETv4.

port.description: Port is the source port or port range to match.

enum.name: Reject
enum.description: Drop matching traffic and send a rejection response.

enum.name: Custom
enum.description: Match the explicit connection states listed in connectionStates.

appCategoryIds.description: AppCategoryIds lists application category IDs to match (when matchingTarget=APP_CATEGORY).

appIds.description: AppIds lists application IDs to match (when matchingTarget=APP).

ipGroupId.description: IpGroupId references a saved IP group as the destination (when matchingTarget=IP, matchingTargetType=OBJECT).

ips.description: Ips lists inline destination IPv4 addresses (when matchingTarget=IP, matchingTargetType=SPECIFIC).

matchOppositeIps.description: MatchOppositeIps inverts (negates) the destination IP match.

matchOppositePorts.description: MatchOppositePorts inverts (negates) the destination port match.

matchingTarget.description: What the destination matches: ANY | APP | APP_CATEGORY | IP | REGION | WEB.

matchingTargetType.description: Refines the destination match: ANY | OBJECT (saved group) | SPECIFIC (inline values).

port.description: Port is the destination port or port range/list (e.g. "80", "1000-2000", "80,443").

portGroupId.description: PortGroupId references a saved port group as the destination ports.

portMatchingType.description: PortMatchingType selects how destination ports match: ANY | SPECIFIC | OBJECT.

regions.description: Regions lists geographic regions to match (when matchingTarget=REGION).

webDomains.description: WebDomains lists web domains to match (when matchingTarget=WEB).

zoneId.description: ZoneId is the controller ID of the destination firewall zone.

enum.name: Both
enum.description: Match both IPv4 and IPv6 traffic.

connectionStates.description: ConnectionStates lists states to match when connectionStateType=CUSTOM: ESTABLISHED | NEW | RELATED | INVALID.

ipVersion.description: L3 version the policy matches: BOTH | IPV4 | IPV6.

matchIpSec.description: MatchIpSec enables matching on IPsec-encapsulated traffic.

protocol.description: Protocol filters by IP protocol: all | tcp_udp | tcp | udp | icmp | icmpv6 | igmp | esp | ah | gre | ... (see UniFi docs).

date.description: Date is a single enforcement date in YYYY-MM-DD format (mode=ONE_TIME_ONLY).

dateEnd.description: DateEnd is the range end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

dateStart.description: DateStart is the range start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

mode.description: Schedule pattern: ALWAYS | EVERY_DAY | EVERY_WEEK | ONE_TIME_ONLY | CUSTOM.

repeatOnDays.description: RepeatOnDays lists recurring days: mon | tue | wed | thu | fri | sat | sun (mode=EVERY_WEEK/CUSTOM).

timeAllDay.description: TimeAllDay applies the policy for the entire day (ignores the time range).

timeRangeEnd.description: TimeRangeEnd is the daily end time in HH:MM (24-hour) format.

timeRangeStart.description: TimeRangeStart is the daily start time in HH:MM (24-hour) format.

enum.name: OneTimeOnly
enum.description: Active once on a single date.

clientMacs.description: ClientMacs lists source client MAC addresses (when matchingTarget=CLIENT).

ipGroupId.description: IpGroupId references a saved IP group as the source (when matchingTarget=IP, matchingTargetType=OBJECT).

ips.description: Ips lists inline source IPv4 addresses (when matchingTarget=IP, matchingTargetType=SPECIFIC).

mac.description: Mac is a single source MAC address (when matchingTarget=MAC).

matchMac.description: MatchMac toggles MAC-based matching of the source.

matchOppositeIps.description: MatchOppositeIps inverts (negates) the source IP match.

matchOppositeNetworks.description: MatchOppositeNetworks inverts (negates) the source network match.

matchOppositePorts.description: MatchOppositePorts inverts (negates) the source port match.

matchingTarget.description: What the source matches: ANY | CLIENT | NETWORK | IP | MAC.

matchingTargetType.description: Refines the source match: OBJECT (saved group) | SPECIFIC (inline values).

networkIds.description: NetworkIds lists the source network IDs (when matchingTarget=NETWORK).

port.description: Port is the source port or port range/list (e.g. "80", "1000-2000", "80,443").

portGroupId.description: PortGroupId references a saved port group as the source ports.

portMatchingType.description: PortMatchingType selects how source ports match: ANY | SPECIFIC | OBJECT.

zoneId.description: ZoneId is the controller ID of the source firewall zone.

querierAddress.description: QuerierAddress is the IGMP querier IPv4 address.

switchMac.description: SwitchMac is the MAC address of the switch acting as querier.

ipAddress.description: IpAddress is the single outbound NAT source IPv4 address.

ipAddressPool.description: IpAddressPool is a list of outbound NAT source IPs/ranges.

mode.description: Outbound NAT strategy: all | ip_address | ip_address_pool.

wanNetworkGroup.description: WAN interface group the mapping applies to: WAN | WAN2.

optionNumber.description: OptionNumber is the DHCP option code (1-254).

downloadKilobitsPerSecond.description: DownloadKilobitsPerSecond is the ISP advertised download rate in kbps.

uploadKilobitsPerSecond.description: UploadKilobitsPerSecond is the ISP advertised upload rate in kbps.

destinationIp.description: DestinationIp is the public/destination IPv4 address this entry matches, or "any".

interface.description: WAN interface this destination IP binds to: wan | wan2.

enum.name: TcpUdp
enum.description: Match both TCP and UDP traffic.

ctrl.description: Ctrl is the 802.1X PNAC mode: auto | force_authorized | force_unauthorized | mac_based | multi_host. Defaults to "force_authorized".

idleTimeout.description: IdleTimeout is the MAC-based 802.1X idle timeout in seconds (0-65535). Defaults to 300.

enabled.description: Enabled enables outbound rate limiting. Defaults to false.

kbps.description: Kbps is the outbound rate limit in kbps (64-9999999). Only applied when enabled is true.

autoneg.description: Autoneg enables auto-negotiation of speed/duplex. Defaults to true. When true it overrides the manual speed and fullDuplex settings.

fecMode.description: FecMode is the forward error correction mode: rs-fec | fc-fec | default | disabled.

fullDuplex.description: FullDuplex enables full-duplex when autoneg is false. Defaults to false.

speed.description: Speed is the fixed port speed in Mbps when autoneg is false: 10 | 100 | 1000 | 2500 | 5000 | 10000 | 20000 | 25000 | 40000 | 50000 | 100000.

enabled.description: Enabled enables LLDP-MED. Defaults to true.

notifyEnabled.description: NotifyEnabled enables LLDP-MED topology-change notifications. Defaults to false.

enabled.description: Enabled enables MAC-based port security. Defaults to false.

macAddresses.description: MacAddresses lists allowed MAC addresses when port security is on.

queue1Level.description: Queue1Level is the QoS priority queue 1 level (0-100).

queue2Level.description: Queue2Level is the QoS priority queue 2 level (0-100).

queue3Level.description: Queue3Level is the QoS priority queue 3 level (0-100).

queue4Level.description: Queue4Level is the QoS priority queue 4 level (0-100).

broadcastEnabled.description: BroadcastEnabled enables broadcast storm control. Defaults to false.

broadcastLevel.description: BroadcastLevel is the broadcast storm-control level (0-100).

broadcastRate.description: BroadcastRate is the broadcast rate in pps (0-14880000).

multicastEnabled.description: MulticastEnabled enables multicast storm control. Defaults to false.

multicastLevel.description: MulticastLevel is the multicast storm-control level (0-100).

multicastRate.description: MulticastRate is the multicast rate in pps (0-14880000).

type.description: Type selects the storm-control metric: level | rate.

unknownUnicastEnabled.description: UnknownUnicastEnabled enables unknown-unicast storm control. Defaults to false.

unknownUnicastLevel.description: UnknownUnicastLevel is the unknown-unicast storm-control level (0-100).

unknownUnicastRate.description: UnknownUnicastRate is the unknown-unicast rate in pps (0-14880000).

excludedNetworkIds.description: ExcludedNetworkIds lists network `_id`s to exclude when forward is "customize".

forward.description: Forward sets the VLAN forwarding mode: all (trunk) | native (access) | customize (selective trunk, use with excludedNetworkIds) | disabled. Defaults to "native".

multicastRouterNetworkIds.description: MulticastRouterNetworkIds lists network `_id`s acting as multicast routers.

nativeNetworkId.description: NativeNetworkId is the network `_id` used as the native (untagged) VLAN.

taggedVlanMgmt.description: TaggedVlanMgmt controls tagged VLAN behavior: auto | block_all | custom.

voiceNetworkId.description: VoiceNetworkId is the network `_id` used for VoIP (voice VLAN) traffic.

enum.name: NexthopRoute
enum.description: Route via a next-hop IP address.

bootFilename.description: BootFilename is the boot file name handed to PXE clients.

bootServer.description: BootServer is the next-server (boot server) IPv4 address.

conflictChecking.description: ConflictChecking probes for IP conflicts before leasing.

dns1.description: Dns1 is the first DHCP-advertised DNS server.

dns2.description: Dns2 is the second DHCP-advertised DNS server.

dns3.description: Dns3 is the third DHCP-advertised DNS server.

dns4.description: Dns4 is the fourth DHCP-advertised DNS server.

dnsEnabled.description: DnsEnabled advertises custom DNS servers via DHCP (otherwise the gateway is used).

enabled.description: Enabled toggles the built-in DHCP server for this network.

gateway.description: Gateway overrides the DHCP-advertised gateway address.

gatewayEnabled.description: GatewayEnabled toggles the custom DHCP gateway override.

guardEnabled.description: GuardEnabled blocks rogue DHCP servers on this network.

lease.description: Lease is the DHCP lease time in seconds (default 86400).

ntp1.description: Ntp1 is the first DHCP-advertised NTP server.

ntp2.description: Ntp2 is the second DHCP-advertised NTP server.

ntpEnabled.description: NtpEnabled advertises NTP servers via DHCP.

relayEnabled.description: RelayEnabled forwards DHCP requests to an external relay instead of serving locally.

start.description: Start is the first address of the DHCP range, e.g. 192.168.20.6.

stop.description: Stop is the last address of the DHCP range, e.g. 192.168.20.254.

tftpServer.description: TftpServer is the TFTP server advertised via DHCP option 66.

timeOffset.description: TimeOffset is the DHCP time offset (option 2) in seconds.

timeOffsetEnabled.description: TimeOffsetEnabled toggles advertising the time offset.

unifiController.description: UnifiController advertises the UniFi controller (inform) address via DHCP.

wins1.description: Wins1 is the first DHCP-advertised WINS server.

wins2.description: Wins2 is the second DHCP-advertised WINS server.

winsEnabled.description: WinsEnabled advertises WINS servers via DHCP.

wpadUrl.description: WpadUrl advertises a WPAD/proxy-autoconfig URL via DHCP.

dns1.description: Dns1 is the first DHCPv6-advertised DNS server.

dns2.description: Dns2 is the second DHCPv6-advertised DNS server.

dns3.description: Dns3 is the third DHCPv6-advertised DNS server.

dns4.description: Dns4 is the fourth DHCPv6-advertised DNS server.

dnsAuto.description: DnsAuto uses upstream-provided DNS for DHCPv6 (default true) instead of manual servers.

enabled.description: Enabled enables the stateful DHCPv6 server.

lease.description: Lease is the DHCPv6 lease time in seconds (default 86400).

start.description: Start is the first address of the DHCPv6 range.

stop.description: Stop is the last address of the DHCPv6 range.

groupMembership.description: GroupMembership is the IGMP group membership interval (seconds).

maxResponse.description: MaxResponse is the IGMP max response time (seconds).

mcrtrExpireTime.description: McrtrExpireTime is the multicast router expiry time (seconds).

proxyDownstreamNetworkIds.description: ProxyDownstreamNetworkIds lists downstream networks when proxyFor=some.

proxyFor.description: ProxyFor selects downstream proxy scope: all | some | none.

proxyUpstream.description: ProxyUpstream marks this network as the IGMP proxy upstream.

querierSwitches.description: QuerierSwitches pins IGMP querier addresses to specific switches.

snooping.description: Snooping enables IGMP snooping to optimize multicast flooding.

suppression.description: Suppression suppresses redundant IGMP membership reports.

pdAutoPrefixIdEnabled.description: PdAutoPrefixIdEnabled lets the controller auto-assign the PD prefix ID.

pdInterface.description: PdInterface is the WAN used for prefix delegation: wan | wan2.

pdPrefixId.description: PdPrefixId is the hex prefix ID carved from the delegated prefix.

pdStart.description: PdStart is the first address of the PD-derived range.

pdStop.description: PdStop is the last address of the PD-derived range.

raPreferredLifetime.description: RaPreferredLifetime is the RA preferred lifetime in seconds (default 14400).

raValidLifetime.description: RaValidLifetime is the RA valid lifetime in seconds (default 86400).

singleNetworkInterface.description: SingleNetworkInterface is the source network for single_network IPv6 mode.

subnet.description: Subnet is the static IPv6 subnet (CIDR) when interfaceType=static.

masquerade.description: Masquerade enables source NAT (masquerade) for this network.

outboundIpAddresses.description: OutboundIpAddresses configures outbound (source) NAT IP mappings.

dhcpCos.description: DhcpCos is the 802.1p CoS applied to WAN DHCP traffic (0-7).

dhcpOptions.description: DhcpOptions are custom DHCP options requested on the WAN.

dhcpv6PdSize.description: Dhcpv6PdSize is the IPv6 PD size to request from the ISP (48-64).

dns1.description: Dns1 is the first WAN DNS server.

dns2.description: Dns2 is the second WAN DNS server.

dns3.description: Dns3 is the third WAN DNS server.

dns4.description: Dns4 is the fourth WAN DNS server.

dsliteRemoteHost.description: DsliteRemoteHost is the DS-Lite AFTR remote host (type=dslite).

egressQos.description: EgressQos is the 802.1p priority for WAN egress (1-7).

gateway.description: Gateway is the static WAN IPv4 gateway.

gatewayV6.description: GatewayV6 is the static WAN IPv6 gateway.

ip.description: Ip is the static WAN IPv4 address.

ipAliases.description: IpAliases are additional WAN IP aliases (CIDR).

ipv6.description: Ipv6 is the static WAN IPv6 address.

ipv6Dns1.description: Ipv6Dns1 is the first WAN IPv6 DNS server.

ipv6Dns2.description: Ipv6Dns2 is the second WAN IPv6 DNS server.

loadBalanceWeight.description: LoadBalanceWeight is the weighted load-balance weight (1-99).

netmask.description: Netmask is the static WAN IPv4 netmask.

networkGroup.description: NetworkGroup is the WAN interface group: WAN | WAN2 | WAN_LTE_FAILOVER.

password.description: Password is the PPPoE password (type=pppoe). Secret.

pppoePasswordEnabled.description: PppoePasswordEnabled toggles sending the PPPoE password.

pppoeUsernameEnabled.description: PppoeUsernameEnabled toggles sending the PPPoE username.

prefixlen.description: Prefixlen is the static WAN IPv6 prefix length (1-128).

providerCapabilities.description: ProviderCapabilities advertises the ISP plan rates for SmartQueue.

smartqDownRate.description: SmartqDownRate is the SmartQueue download limit in kbps.

smartqEnabled.description: SmartqEnabled enables SmartQueue QoS on the WAN.

smartqUpRate.description: SmartqUpRate is the SmartQueue upload limit in kbps.

type.description: Type (purpose=wan): disabled | dhcp | static | pppoe | dslite.

vlan.description: Vlan tags the WAN interface with a VLAN ID. Setting it enables WAN VLAN tagging.

vlanEnabled.description: VlanEnabled toggles WAN VLAN tagging explicitly.

ids.description: Ids are the AP groups that should broadcast this SSID.

mode.description: Mode controls AP selection: all | groups | devices.

enum.name: Groups
enum.description: Broadcast on the listed AP groups.

band.description: Band is the radio band: 2g | 5g | both.

bands.description: Bands are the radio bands to broadcast on: 2g | 5g | 6g.

no2GhzOui.description: No2GhzOui steers high-performance clients to 5GHz only.

enabled.description: Enabled enables deep packet inspection for this WLAN.

groupId.description: GroupId is the DPI group to apply.

mode.description: Mode controls DTIM interval handling: default | custom.

na.description: Na is the DTIM interval for the 5GHz band (1-255).

ng.description: Ng is the DTIM interval for the 2.4GHz band (1-255).

sixE.description: SixE is the DTIM interval for the 6GHz band (1-255).

enhanced.description: Enhanced enables enhanced IoT connectivity behaviors.

optimizeWifiConnectivity.description: OptimizeWifiConnectivity optimizes connectivity for IoT devices.

enabled.description: Enabled enables MAC-based access control.

list.description: List is the list of MACs (XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX) the policy applies to.

naDataRateKbps.description: NaDataRateKbps is the minimum 5GHz data rate in Kbps.

naEnabled.description: NaEnabled enables the 5GHz minimum data rate control.

ngDataRateKbps.description: NgDataRateKbps is the minimum 2.4GHz data rate in Kbps.

ngEnabled.description: NgEnabled enables the 2.4GHz minimum data rate control.

broadcastFilterEnabled.description: BroadcastFilterEnabled enables filtering of broadcast/multicast traffic.

broadcastFilterList.description: BroadcastFilterList is the allow list of MACs exempt from filtering.

enhanceEnabled.description: EnhanceEnabled converts multicast to unicast for reliability.

proxyArp.description: ProxyArp lets APs proxy common broadcast frames as unicast.

crossConnect.description: CrossConnect allows P2P traffic across APs.

enabled.description: Enabled enables peer-to-peer (client-to-client) traffic.

enum.name: Auto
enum.description: Let the controller choose the PMF cipher.

enabled.description: Enabled enables per-key network placement.

keys.description: Keys is the list of private pre-shared keys.

networkId.description: NetworkId is the network/VLAN (`_id`) clients using this key are placed on.

password.description: Password is the pre-shared key for this entry (8-255 chars). Secret.

macAclEmptyPassword.description: MacAclEmptyPassword sends an empty password for MAC ACL auth.

macAclFormat.description: MacAclFormat is the MAC ACL format: none_lower | hyphen_lower | colon_lower | none_upper | hyphen_upper | colon_upper.

nasIdentifier.description: NasIdentifier is the RADIUS NAS identifier value (0-48 chars).

nasIdentifierType.description: NasIdentifierType: ap_name | ap_mac | bssid | site_name | custom.

profileId.description: ProfileId is the RADIUS profile (`_id`) for wpaeap security.

iappKey.description: IappKey is the inter-AP protocol key (32 hex chars). Secret.

antiClogging.description: AntiClogging is the SAE anti-clogging threshold.

groups.description: Groups are the SAE finite cyclic groups to allow.

psks.description: Psks is the list of WPA3 SAE pre-shared keys.

sync.description: Sync is the SAE sync value.

id.description: Id is an optional identifier for this SAE PSK entry.

mac.description: Mac optionally binds this key to a specific client MAC (XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX).

psk.description: Psk is the SAE pre-shared key (8-255 chars). Secret.

vlan.description: Vlan optionally places clients using this key onto the given VLAN ID.

enabled.description: Enabled enables time-based broadcast scheduling.

entries.description: Entries is the duration-based broadcast schedule.

legacy.description: Legacy is the legacy schedule format entries (day|HHMM-HHMM).

durationMinutes.description: DurationMinutes is how long, in minutes, the SSID stays active once started.

name.description: Name is an optional friendly label for the schedule entry.

enum.name: WpaPsk
enum.description: WPA personal with a pre-shared key.

vlan.description: Vlan is the VLAN ID to tag client traffic with.

key.description: Key is the WEP key. Secret. Only used when security is "wep".

enc.description: Enc is the WPA encryption cipher: auto | ccmp | gcmp | ccmp-256 | gcmp-256.

groupRekey.description: GroupRekey is the group key rekey interval in seconds (0 disables).

mode.description: Mode is the WPA mode: auto | wpa1 | wpa2.

pmfCipher.description: PmfCipher is the PMF cipher: auto | aes-128-cmac | bip-gmac-256.

pmfMode.description: PmfMode is Protected Management Frames mode: disabled | optional | required.

pskRadius.description: PskRadius controls RADIUS PSK auth: disabled | optional | required. This is a mode enum, not a credential, so it is not marked secret.

fastRoaming.description: FastRoaming enables 802.11r fast roaming for WPA3.

support.description: Support enables WPA3 (requires wpapsk security and PMF enabled).

transition.description: Transition enables WPA3/WPA2 transition mode (requires support).

enum.name: Auto

type.description: Type is the match type. Defaults to "is".

value.description: Value refines some sources (e.g. a crossing-line direction or a known license plate).

enabled.description: Enabled toggles the cooldown.

timeoutMs.description: TimeoutMs is the suppression window in milliseconds.

device.description: Device is the device MAC address, uppercase hex without separators (e.g. "F4E2C6730625").

Owns a single (share, client) entry in the appliance's global NFS export list; writes are read-modify-writes serialised within the provider process. Requires the global NFS service to be enabled on the appliance.

client.description: Client is the NFS client IP or CIDR allowed to mount the drive.

permission.description: Permission is the access level: "rw" (default) or "ro".

shareName.description: ShareName is the exported drive's name (resolved from ShareId).

quotaGib.description: QuotaGib is the size limit in gibibytes. Omit (or <= 0) for no quota.

unifi:network:Device.description: Manage settings of an existing, already-adopted UniFi network device (gateway, switch, access point, PDU). deviceId.description: DeviceId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

ethernetOverrides.description: EthernetOverrides assigns network groups to physical ethernet ports (gateways).

lcm.description: Lcm groups the front LCD/touchscreen display settings.

led.description: Led groups the status-LED indicator settings.

mac.description: Mac is the device MAC address (required). The device must already be adopted on the controller.

mgmtNetworkId.description: MgmtNetworkId pins the device's management network (the network's `_id`).

model.description: Model is the device model code, e.g. "USPRPS" or "U6-Enterprise" (read-only).

name.description: Name is the device's display name.

outlet.description: Outlet groups the PDU outlet settings.

portOverrides.description: PortOverrides configures individual switch ports (the centerpiece for switches).

snmp.description: Snmp groups the SNMP agent strings.

state.description: State is the connection state, e.g. "Connected", "Pending" (read-only).

stp.description: Stp groups the spanning-tree bridge settings.

switching.description: Switching groups the switch-wide L2 settings.

type.description: Type is the device type, e.g. "usw", "uap", "ugw" (read-only).

vrrp.description: Vrrp groups the gateway VRRP high-availability settings.

unifi:network:DnsRecord.description: A UniFi controller-local (static) DNS record. Supported on gateways such as the UDM-SE. Maps to a controller static-dns object.

dnsRecordId.description: DnsRecordId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the record is active. Defaults to true.

key.description: Key is the hostname/record name the record answers for, e.g. "host.example.com".

port.description: Port is the target port for SRV records.

priority.description: Priority is the preference value for MX and SRV records.

recordType.description: RecordType is the DNS record type: A | AAAA | CNAME | MX | NS | PTR | SOA | SRV | TXT.

ttl.description: Ttl is the record time-to-live in seconds. 0 lets the controller pick a default.

value.description: Value is the record payload (e.g. an IP for A/AAAA, a hostname for CNAME/MX/NS).

weight.description: Weight is the relative weight for SRV records.

unifi:network:FirewallGroup.description: A UniFi firewall group: a reusable set of addresses or ports referenced by firewall rules.

firewallGroupId.description: FirewallGroupId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

groupMembers.description: GroupMembers are the entries in the group. The accepted format depends on GroupType: IPv4 addresses/CIDRs for address-group, port numbers/ranges for port-group, or IPv6 addresses/CIDRs for ipv6-address-group.

groupType.description: GroupType selects the kind of group: address-group | port-group | ipv6-address-group.

name.description: Name of the firewall group (1-64 characters).

unifi:network:FirewallRule.description: A classic (per-ruleset) UniFi firewall rule. Rules are evaluated in order within a ruleset, controlled by ruleIndex. Use firewall groups, networks, addresses, ports and connection-state matching to permit or block traffic. Match selectors are grouped into nested objects (protocolMatch, source, destination, connectionState).

action.description: Action taken on matching traffic: accept | drop | reject.

connectionState.description: ConnectionState groups the conntrack-state match toggles.

destination.description: Destination groups the destination-side match selectors.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the rule is active. Defaults to true.

firewallRuleId.description: FirewallRuleId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

ipSec.description: IpSec matches on IPsec encapsulation: match-ipsec | match-none.

logging.description: Logging enables logging of packets that match this rule.

name.description: Name of the firewall rule (1-128 characters).

protocolMatch.description: ProtocolMatch groups the protocol / ICMP-type match selectors.

ruleIndex.description: RuleIndex is the ordering index of the rule within its ruleset. Must be >= 2000 and < 3000, or >= 4000 and < 5000.

ruleset.description: Ruleset the rule belongs to, from the perspective of the security gateway: WAN_IN | WAN_OUT | WAN_LOCAL | LAN_IN | LAN_OUT | LAN_LOCAL | GUEST_IN | GUEST_OUT | GUEST_LOCAL | WANv6_IN | WANv6_OUT | WANv6_LOCAL | LANv6_IN | LANv6_OUT | LANv6_LOCAL | GUESTv6_IN | GUESTv6_OUT | GUESTv6_LOCAL.

settingPreference.description: SettingPreference controls whether the rule uses automatic or manual settings: auto | manual.

source.description: Source groups the source-side match selectors.

unifi:network:FirewallZonePolicy.description: A UniFi zone-based firewall policy (the Zone-Based Firewall feature on current UDM/UDM-SE/UXG firmware). Each policy matches traffic flowing from a source zone to a destination zone and applies an action. This resource requires a controller with the zone-based firewall enabled; it is not available on older controllers that still use the legacy firewall rule list.

action.description: Action is the traffic disposition: ALLOW | BLOCK | REJECT.

description.description: Description is free-form documentation for the policy.

destination.description: Destination is the destination zone and matching criteria.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the policy is active. Defaults to true.

firewallZonePolicyId.description: FirewallZonePolicyId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

index.description: Index is the policy priority/ordering rank (lower is evaluated first).

logging.description: Logging enables syslog logging for traffic matching this policy.

matching.description: Matching groups the flow-level traffic-match criteria (protocol, IP version, connection-state, and IPsec selection).

name.description: Name is the policy identifier shown in the controller.

schedule.description: Schedule is the optional temporal enforcement window. When unset the policy is always active.

unifi:network:PortForward.description: A UniFi port-forwarding (destination NAT) rule. Forwards traffic arriving on a WAN port (dstPort) to an internal host (fwd) and port (fwdPort).

destinationIp.description: DestinationIp is the public/destination IPv4 address this rule matches, or "any".

destinationIps.description: DestinationIps maps destination IPs to specific WAN interfaces for multi-WAN setups.

dstPort.description: DstPort is the external/WAN port traffic arrives ON, i.e. forwarded FROM (single port or range).

unifi:network:PortProfile.description: A UniFi switch port profile (portconf). A reusable collection of port settings grouped into nested objects (vlan, link, stormControl, portSecurity, dot1x, lldpMed, egressRateLimit, priorityQueues) that can be applied to switch ports via a Device port override's portProfileId.

egressRateLimit.description: EgressRateLimit groups the outbound rate-limiting settings.

isolation.description: Isolation enables port isolation so devices on this profile cannot communicate with each other. Defaults to false.

link.description: Link groups the physical-layer link settings (speed/duplex/FEC).

lldpMed.description: LldpMed groups the LLDP-MED protocol toggles.

name.description: Name is a descriptive name for the port profile.

opMode.description: OpMode is the operation mode. Only "switch" is supported. Defaults to "switch".

poeMode.description: PoeMode controls Power-over-Ethernet: auto | off (the values go-unifi accepts for a port profile). Per-port passthrough/pasv24 modes are set via a Device port override's poeMode, not here.

portKeepaliveEnabled.description: PortKeepaliveEnabled enables port keepalive. Defaults to false.

portProfileId.description: PortProfileId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`), referenced by a Device port override's portProfileId (portconf_id).

portSecurity.description: PortSecurity groups the MAC-based port-security settings.

priorityQueues.description: PriorityQueues groups the QoS priority-queue levels.

settingPreference.description: SettingPreference controls config source: auto | manual.

stpPortMode.description: StpPortMode enables Spanning Tree Protocol on the port. Defaults to true.

unifi:network:StaticRoute.description: A UniFi static route. Maps to a controller routing object with type=static-route, directing traffic for a destination network via a next hop, an egress interface, or a blackhole.

distance.description: Distance is the administrative distance / metric of the route (1-255).

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the route is active. Defaults to true.

gatewayDevice.description: GatewayDevice is the MAC address of the gateway device that hosts the route.

gatewayType.description: GatewayType selects which gateway handles the route: default | switch.

unifi:network:User.description: A UniFi user (a known client / device). Maps to a controller user object keyed by MAC address. Creating this resource registers the client on the controller; deleting it removes the user record. Supports assigning a friendly name, user group, fixed IP, local DNS record, virtual network (VLAN) override, fixed access point, and blocking the client.

blocked.description: Blocked, when true, blocks this client from accessing the network.

devIdOverride.description: DevIdOverride overrides the detected device fingerprint (device type id). 0 clears the override.

fixedApEnabled.description: FixedApEnabled toggles pinning the client to a fixed access point. Defaults to true when FixedApMac is set.

fixedApMac.description: FixedApMac is the MAC address of the access point the client is pinned to.

fixedIp.description: FixedIp is a static IPv4 address to assign to this client. Setting it enables UseFixedIp.

hostname.description: Hostname is the hostname the controller has observed for the client.

ip.description: Ip is the last-known IP address of the client (best-effort; may be empty).

lastSeen.description: LastSeen is the Unix timestamp (seconds) the client was last seen.

localDnsRecord.description: LocalDnsRecord is a local DNS hostname resolving to this client. Setting it enables LocalDnsRecordEnabled.

localDnsRecordEnabled.description: LocalDnsRecordEnabled toggles publishing the LocalDnsRecord. Defaults to true when LocalDnsRecord is set.

mac.description: Mac is the client MAC address (e.g. "00:11:22:33:44:55"). It is the unique key used to register and look up the client.

name.description: Name is a friendly name for the client.

networkId.description: NetworkId is the ID of the network the fixed IP is assigned from.

note.description: Note is free-form text stored alongside the client.

useFixedIp.description: UseFixedIp toggles assigning the FixedIp to the client. Defaults to true when FixedIp is set.

userGroupId.description: UserGroupId is the ID of the user group (bandwidth profile) this client belongs to.

userId.description: UserId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

virtualNetworkOverrideEnabled.description: VirtualNetworkOverrideEnabled toggles overriding the client's virtual network (VLAN). Defaults to true when VirtualNetworkOverrideId is set.

virtualNetworkOverrideId.description: VirtualNetworkOverrideId is the ID of the virtual network (VLAN) the client is pinned to.

blocked.description: Blocked, when true, blocks this client from accessing the network.

devIdOverride.description: DevIdOverride overrides the detected device fingerprint (device type id). 0 clears the override.

fixedApEnabled.description: FixedApEnabled toggles pinning the client to a fixed access point. Defaults to true when FixedApMac is set.

fixedApMac.description: FixedApMac is the MAC address of the access point the client is pinned to.

fixedIp.description: FixedIp is a static IPv4 address to assign to this client. Setting it enables UseFixedIp.

localDnsRecord.description: LocalDnsRecord is a local DNS hostname resolving to this client. Setting it enables LocalDnsRecordEnabled.

localDnsRecordEnabled.description: LocalDnsRecordEnabled toggles publishing the LocalDnsRecord. Defaults to true when LocalDnsRecord is set.

mac.description: Mac is the client MAC address (e.g. "00:11:22:33:44:55"). It is the unique key used to register and look up the client.

name.description: Name is a friendly name for the client.

networkId.description: NetworkId is the ID of the network the fixed IP is assigned from.

note.description: Note is free-form text stored alongside the client.

useFixedIp.description: UseFixedIp toggles assigning the FixedIp to the client. Defaults to true when FixedIp is set.

userGroupId.description: UserGroupId is the ID of the user group (bandwidth profile) this client belongs to.

virtualNetworkOverrideEnabled.description: VirtualNetworkOverrideEnabled toggles overriding the client's virtual network (VLAN). Defaults to true when VirtualNetworkOverrideId is set.

virtualNetworkOverrideId.description: VirtualNetworkOverrideId is the ID of the virtual network (VLAN) the client is pinned to.

unifi:network:UserGroup.description: A UniFi user group, applying per-client bandwidth (QoS rate) limits that can be assigned to clients.

name.description: Name of the user group.

qosRateMaxDown.description: QosRateMaxDown is the maximum download rate in kbps. -1 means unlimited. Defaults to -1.

qosRateMaxUp.description: QosRateMaxUp is the maximum upload rate in kbps. -1 means unlimited. Defaults to -1.

userGroupId.description: UserGroupId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

name.description: Name of the user group.

unifi:network:Vlan.description: A UniFi Network (VLAN/LAN/WAN). Maps to a controller network configuration object. Covers LAN/VLAN DHCP (v4 and v6), IGMP, IPv6, and WAN settings, grouped into nested objects (dhcp, dhcpV6, ipv6, igmp, wan, nat). VPN-specific networks (IPSec/OpenVPN/WireGuard/L2TP/PPTP) are not modeled.

autoScaleEnabled.description: AutoScaleEnabled lets the controller auto-size the subnet.

dhcp.description: Dhcp groups the IPv4 DHCP-server settings.

dhcpV6.description: DhcpV6 groups the stateful DHCPv6-server settings.

domainName.description: DomainName is the DNS domain handed to clients.

dpiEnabled.description: DpiEnabled enables Deep Packet Inspection on this network.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the network is active. Defaults to true.

igmp.description: Igmp groups the multicast / IGMP settings.

interfaceMtu.description: InterfaceMtu sets the interface MTU. Setting it enables the MTU override.

interfaceMtuEnabled.description: InterfaceMtuEnabled toggles the interface MTU override explicitly.

internetAccessEnabled.description: InternetAccessEnabled controls whether clients may reach the internet. Defaults to true.

ipv6.description: Ipv6 groups the IPv6 addressing / RA / prefix-delegation settings.

macOverride.description: MacOverride clones a MAC address on the interface (commonly used on WAN).

macOverrideEnabled.description: MacOverrideEnabled toggles the MAC clone override.

mdnsEnabled.description: MdnsEnabled enables multicast DNS (Bonjour/mDNS) repeating on this network.

name.description: Name of the network.

nat.description: Nat groups the source/outbound NAT settings.

networkGroup.description: NetworkGroup is the interface group: LAN, LAN2..LAN8 (or WAN/WAN2 for WAN networks). Defaults to "LAN".

networkId.description: NetworkId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

networkIsolationEnabled.description: NetworkIsolationEnabled isolates clients from other networks. Defaults to false.

purpose.description: Purpose: corporate | guest | vlan-only | wan | remote-user-vpn | site-vpn | vpn-client. Defaults to "corporate".

settingPreference.description: SettingPreference: auto | manual. Controls whether device-specific overrides apply.

subnet.description: Subnet is the gateway IP/CIDR for the network, e.g. 192.168.20.1/24.

upnpLanEnabled.description: UpnpLanEnabled enables UPnP on this LAN.

vlan.description: Vlan is the 802.1Q VLAN ID. When set, VLAN tagging is enabled.

wan.description: Wan groups the WAN-uplink settings (used when purpose=wan).

unifi:network:Wlan.description: A UniFi wireless network (SSID). Maps to a controller wlanconf object. Security, RADIUS, roaming, band/rate, scheduling, and other settings are grouped into nested objects (wpa, wpa3, sae, wep, radius, roaming, etc.).

apGroups.description: ApGroups groups which AP groups/devices broadcast this SSID.

bandSteering.description: BandSteering groups radio band selection and band steering.

dpi.description: Dpi groups the Deep Packet Inspection toggle and group reference.

dtim.description: Dtim groups the DTIM interval control across bands.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the SSID is broadcast. Defaults to true.

hideSsid.description: HideSsid hides the SSID from broadcast. Defaults to false.

iot.description: Iot groups the IoT connectivity behaviors.

isGuest.description: IsGuest marks this WLAN as a guest network (enables guest behaviors).

l2Isolation.description: L2Isolation isolates wireless clients from each other at layer 2.

macFilter.description: MacFilter groups the MAC access-control list.

multicast.description: Multicast groups the multicast/broadcast traffic handling.

name.description: Name is the SSID.

networkId.description: NetworkId binds the WLAN to a network/VLAN (the network's `_id`).

p2p.description: P2p groups the peer-to-peer (client-to-client) traffic settings.

passphrase.description: Passphrase is the WPA pre-shared key (8-63 chars). Secret.

roaming.description: Roaming groups the general client roaming/handoff settings.

sae.description: Sae groups the WPA3 SAE handshake configuration.

schedule.description: Schedule groups the time-based broadcast scheduling.

security.description: Security: open | wpapsk | wpaeap | wep | osen. Defaults to "wpapsk".

uapsdEnabled.description: UapsdEnabled enables Unscheduled Automatic Power Save Delivery.

userGroupId.description: UserGroupId is the user group (rate limiting / firewall) for clients.

vlanTagging.description: VlanTagging groups the VLAN tagging facet.

wep.description: Wep groups the legacy WEP-only settings.

wlanGroupId.description: WlanGroupId is the WLAN group to attach to. Required on many controllers.

wlanId.description: WlanId is the controller-assigned identifier (the UniFi `_id`).

wpa.description: Wpa groups the core WPA/encryption tuning.

wpa3.description: Wpa3 groups the WPA3 feature cluster.

unifi:protect:AlarmAutomation.description: A UniFi Protect Alarm Manager rule (sources + conditions -> webhook actions). Uses Protect's private automations API: requires the console's Alarm Manager to be in LOCAL mode, and may require username/password auth. The resource owns the rule's entire actions list.

automationId.description: AutomationId is the controller-assigned rule identifier.

conditions.description: Conditions are the trigger conditions (ANDed). At least one is required.

cooldown.description: Cooldown suppresses repeat fires. Defaults to disabled with a 10-minute window.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the rule fires. Defaults to true.

name.description: Name is the rule's display name.

sources.description: Sources scopes the rule to devices. Empty means all devices.

webhookActions.description: WebhookActions fire when the rule matches. At least one is required.

unifi:protect:Camera.description: Manage settings of an existing UniFi Protect camera (adoption model; cameras are not created or deleted via the API). Exposes the writable fields of the Protect CameraPatchRequest.

cameraId.description: CameraId is the Protect camera ID (from the Protect API / bootstrap).

lcdMessageResetAt.description: LcdMessageResetAt is the epoch-millisecond timestamp at which the LCD message clears. 0 (or omitted) leaves the message until changed.

lcdMessageText.description: LcdMessageText is the doorbell LCD custom message text (used with type CUSTOM_MESSAGE).

lcdMessageType.description: LcdMessageType is the doorbell LCD message type, e.g. "CUSTOM_MESSAGE", "DO_NOT_DISTURB", "LEAVE_PACKAGE_AT_DOOR" (doorbell cameras only).

micVolume.description: MicVolume sets the microphone volume (0-100). A value of 0 is not applied by the Protect API (the field is omitted when zero).

name.description: Name is the camera's display name.

osdDateEnabled.description: OsdDateEnabled overlays the date/time on the video (osdSettings.isDateEnabled).

osdDebugEnabled.description: OsdDebugEnabled overlays debug telemetry on the video (osdSettings.isDebugEnabled).

osdLogoEnabled.description: OsdLogoEnabled overlays the logo on the video (osdSettings.isLogoEnabled).

osdNameEnabled.description: OsdNameEnabled overlays the camera name on the video (osdSettings.isNameEnabled).

smartDetectAudioTypes.description: SmartDetectAudioTypes selects which sounds trigger smart detections, e.g. "smoke", "cmonitor" (CO alarm), "alrmSmoke", "alrmCmonitor".

smartDetectObjectTypes.description: SmartDetectObjectTypes selects which objects trigger smart detections, e.g. "person", "vehicle", "animal", "package", "licensePlate".

state.description: State is the connection state, e.g. "CONNECTED" (read-only).

type.description: Type is the camera model/type (read-only; the Protect modelKey).

videoMode.description: VideoMode selects the capture mode, e.g. "default", "highFps", "sport".
